description:
You are a mobile build engineer who has just checked-in a small, experimental Android build
pipeline under your home directory.  
Security policy requires that the new assets have tightly-scoped UNIX permissions before
the first CI run.  
Your goal is to 1) lock down the three filesystem objects listed below, and 2) create a
very small audit file proving that the permissions were applied correctly.

PRE-EXISTING MATERIAL  
(the files and directories already exist when the session begins)

1. /home/user/mobile-pipeline/build_android.sh  
   • Current mode: 777  
   • First line (shebang) is already present: “#!/bin/bash”  
2. /home/user/mobile-pipeline/logs/            (directory)  
   • Current mode: 755  
3. /home/user/mobile-pipeline/release_signing.key  
   • Current mode: 644  
   • Contains a single ASCII line that looks like a dummy signing key.

YOUR TASK  
A. Permissions  
   1. build_android.sh must be:   rwx for owner, r-x for group, no rights for others  
      (octal 750).  
   2. logs directory must be:     rwx for owner only, nothing for group/others  
      (octal 700).  
   3. release_signing.key must be: rw for owner only, nothing for group/others  
      (octal 600).

B. Audit file  
   After fixing permissions, create: /home/user/mobile-pipeline/permission_audit.log

   Exact required format – plain text, three lines, newline-terminated, **no
   additional spaces**:

     <absolute-path> <octal-mode>  
     <absolute-path> <octal-mode>  
     <absolute-path> <octal-mode>

   where the paths appear **in the following order**:
     1) /home/user/mobile-pipeline/build_android.sh  
     2) /home/user/mobile-pipeline/logs  
     3) /home/user/mobile-pipeline/release_signing.key  

   Example layout (octal shown is just an illustration – do not copy these numbers):

     /home/user/…/build_android.sh 750  
     /home/user/…/logs 700  
     /home/user/…/release_signing.key 600  

   Use an appropriate one-liner (e.g., stat) so the file is machine-verifiable.

CONSTRAINTS  
• Solve the whole task with a simple series of 2–3 shell commands.  
• Do NOT use sudo; you already own the files.  
• Do NOT alter any file contents except permission_audit.log.  
• When finished, the automated grader will:  
  1) read permission_audit.log  
  2) verify that the three octal modes and their order match the spec  
  3) confirm the actual filesystem modes with `stat` against your log.

If everything is correct, you are done.
truth:
Final expected state for automated verification:

Filesystem permissions (octal):
- /home/user/mobile-pipeline/build_android.sh : 750
- /home/user/mobile-pipeline/logs             : 700
- /home/user/mobile-pipeline/release_signing.key : 600

File /home/user/mobile-pipeline/permission_audit.log must exist with permissions 644 (default touch) or stricter, contents EXACTLY:

/home/user/mobile-pipeline/build_android.sh 750
/home/user/mobile-pipeline/logs 700
/home/user/mobile-pipeline/release_signing.key 600

Trailing newline after the final line is required.

The three pre-existing items are present before task starts with looser permissions; only permissions need change, contents remain unchanged.

Automated test sequence:
1. Read the audit file and verify it contains exactly 3 lines identical to above.
2. For each path, run `stat -c "%a"` and compare to required octal.
If both checks pass, task is successful.
